2605.02699.title: Learning Equivariant Neural-Augmented Object Dynamics From Few Interactions
2605.02699.paper_summary: Learning data-efficient object dynamics models for robotic manipulation remains challenging, especially for deformable objects. A popular approach is to model objects as sets of 3D particles and learn their motion using graph neural networks. In practice, this is not enough to maintain physical feasibility over long horizons and may require large amounts of interaction data to learn. We introduce PIEGraph, a novel approach to combining analytical physics and data-driven models to capture object dynamics for both rigid and deformable bodies using limited real-world interaction data. PIEGraph consists of two components: (1) a \textbf{P}hysically \textbf{I}nformed particle-based analytical model (implemented as a spring--mass system) to enforce physically feasible motion, and (2) an \textbf{E}quivariant \textbf{Graph} Neural Network with a novel action representation that exploits symmetries in particle interactions to guide the analytical model. We evaluate PIEGraph in simulation and on robot hardware for reorientation and repositioning tasks with ropes, cloth, stuffed animals and rigid objects. We show that our method enables accurate dynamics prediction and reliable downstream robotic manipulation planning, which outperforms state of the art baselines.
2605.02699.paper_summary_zh: 學習數據高效的物體動力學模型以進行機器人操作仍然具有挑戰性，特別是對於可變形物體。 一種流行的方法是將物體建模為一組3D粒子，並使用圖神經網絡學習它們的運動。 實際上，這不足以在長時間內保持物理可行性，並且可能需要大量的交互數據來進行學習。 我們介紹了PIEGraph，一種新穎的方法，將分析物理學和數據驅動模型結合起來，利用有限的現實世界交互數據捕捉剛性和可變形物體的動力學。 PIEGraph由兩個組件組成： (1) 一個\textbf{P}hysically \textbf{I}nformed基於粒子的分析模型（實現為彈簧-質量系統），以強制執行物理可行的運動，和 (2) 一個\textbf{E}quivariant \textbf{Graph}神經網絡，具有新穎的行動表示，利用粒子交互中的對稱性來指導分析模型。 我們在模擬和機器人硬體上對PIEGraph進行了評估，針對繩索、布料、填充玩具和剛性物體的重新定向和重新定位任務。 我們展示了我們的方法能夠實現準確的動力學預測和可靠的下游機器人操作規劃，超越了最先進的基準。
2605.02699.author: Sergio Orozco et.al.
2605.02699.authors: Sergio Orozco, Tushar Kusnur, Brandon May, George Konidaris, Laura Herlant
